title: Boat Rental in Athens: A Complete Guide to Costs and Choices 2026
html:
Exploring the Athenian Riviera and the Saronic Gulf by boat is straightforward when you use a reputable broker. Depart from Alimos Marina, and a day charter on a five-metre motorboat typically starts from €250 for a half-day, while a crewed catamaran for eight guests runs closer to €1,200. You can compare verified boat listings on BoatHire24, a worldwide marketplace for renting boats and listing your own.

Where to set off in Athens

The main departure points for a boating trip in Athens are concentrated along the southern coast and the nearby islands. From Zea Marina in Piraeus to Marina Flisvos in Paleo Faliro, you have excellent facilities and ferry connections. Alimos Marina is the largest yacht marina in Greece, offering chandlers and fuelling docks. For a quieter start, head to Vouliagmeni Marina or the sandy bay of Agios Kosmas. Day excursions commonly head to the island of Aegina, some 18 nautical miles away, or the smaller coves of Salamina and Agistri.

How much does it cost to rent a boat in Athens?

The daily cost to rent a boat in Athens typically ranges from €200 to €4,500 depending on the vessel type, season, and whether it is crewed or bareboat. To give you a clearer picture, here is a price-band breakdown for 2026:

  • A shared sunset cruise along the Athenian coast costs around €60, 90 per person and lasts three hours.
  • Private half-day rental of a small motorboat (e.g. an Axopar 27) for six guests starts at €350, plus fuel.
  • A full-day catamaran charter for up to twelve people is typically €1,200, 2,200, often including a skipper.
  • A crewed luxury motor yacht (like a Sunseeker 68) for eight guests can exceed €4,000 per day, including crew and fuel.

For an accurate price, you can compare verified boat listings for boat hire Athens; the platform lists real boats with transparent rates.

Which boat to rent

The right boat depends on your group size, budget, and whether you want to captain yourself or have a crew. Here is a ranked list of popular choices in Athens:

  1. Small motorboat (4, 6 m), best for couples or small groups exploring coastal caves; fits 4 guests; from €250 for a half-day.
  2. Day-cruiser (7, 9 m), ideal for families or friends with some boating experience; fits 8 guests; from €450 for a full day.
  3. Sailing catamaran (38, 45 ft), stable and spacious for day trips with up to 12 guests; from €1,000 for a full day, including skipper.
  4. Luxury motor yacht (14, 20 m), for special celebrations or corporate outings, often with a crew; fits up to 10 guests; from €2,500 per day.
  5. Rigid inflatable boat (RIB), fast and nimble for watersports; fits 6 guests; from €300 for a half-day.

For a specific example, see the Azimut 58 listing on BoatHire24, which shows a 17.9-metre flybridge motor yacht available for charter with a professional crew.

Do you need a licence to rent a boat in Athens?

If you rent a bareboat power vessel over 30 hp in Greece, you generally need a Greek powerboat licence or an internationally recognised equivalent. The operator will confirm the exact requirements for your specific boat, as some smaller outboards may be rented without a licence. Sailing yachts and catamarans under a certain length sometimes have different rules. Always check with the listing owner on BoatHire24 before booking.

Local tip: book your boat early for July and August, when the meltemi wind can strengthen in the afternoon. Many experienced renters start their day at 0900 and return by 1500 to avoid choppy seas.

At a glance

  • Price band: €200, 4,500 per day
  • Peak months: July, August (high season) and June, September (shoulder)
  • Trip length: half-day (4 h) or full-day (8 h)
  • Capacity: 4, 12 guests
  • Currency: Euros (EUR)

Questions about boat rental in Athens

Can I rent a boat in Athens without a licence?

Yes, you can rent crewed boats or small outboards under 30 hp without a licence, but the operator decides the exact policy.

What is the best month for sailing in Athens?

May, June, and September offer settled weather, less wind, and fewer crowds than July and August.

How many people can a typical day boat hold in Athens?

Most boats advertised on BoatHire24 accommodate between 6 and 12 guests, which is the standard for day trips in the Saronic Gulf.

Are fuel costs included in the rental price?

Fuel is usually extra for bareboat charters but often included for crewed yachts and catamarans.

BoatHire24 is a worldwide marketplace where you can rent a boat in Athens or list your own. Whether you are a first-time renter or a seasoned captain, the platform provides transparent listings and direct communication with owners. If you own a boat in Athens, consider listing it to reach thousands of travellers planning their 2026 holidays.

Written by the BoatHire24 team, who book and list verified boats in Athens and hundreds of destinations worldwide.
